title: PCL-AD-002
subtitle: Novel Pyrimidine-Morpholine BACE1 Inhibitor Variant
key_features: Pyrimidine-morpholine core targeting BACE1 for Alzheimer's disease
Difluoroaniline moiety enhances BBB penetration for CNS delivery
Morpholine ring improves aqueous solubility and metabolic stability
Low IP risk (max Tanimoto 0.26 vs known drugs) — novel chemical space
design_rationale: Designed as a potential BACE1 inhibitor variant for Alzheimer's disease. The pyrimidine-morpholine scaffold provides dual functionality: the pyrimidine ring offers key hydrogen-bond acceptor sites for target binding, while the morpholine ring enhances solubility and CNS penetration. The 2,4-difluoroaniline substituent was chosen for optimal BBB permeability and metabolic resistance, avoiding known scaffolds like verubecestat.
modifications_suggested: Replace phenyl → pyridyl for improved aqueous solubility
Replace phenyl → pyrimidyl for novel IP + solubility boost
Explore cyclopropyl substitution to reduce LogP further
Add CF3 group for novel IP space with similar lipophilicity
image_b64:
$(python3 -c "
from rdkit import Chem
from rdkit.Chem import Draw
import base64, io
mol = Chem.MolFromSmiles('c1cc(c(cc1F)NC(=O)c2cnc(nc2)N3CCOCC3)F')
img = Draw.MolToImage(mol, size=(400, 400))
buf = io.BytesIO()
img.save(buf, format='PNG')
print(base64.b64encode(buf.getvalue()).decode())
")
